# Livepeer Docs

## Home

### Home

- [Welcome to Livepeer Mission Control](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/home/mission-control): Livepeer powers on-demand real-time video streaming and AI pipelines through a full-stack open hardware network, providing users with affordable, verifiable, permissionless enterprise-grade softwar...
- [Livepeer Primer](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/home/primer): A 10 minute primer on Livepeer, the community, the use cases, technology and where to get started

### Home / Livepeer

- [Livepeer Story](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/home/about-livepeer/vision): How Livepeer began with video streaming and grew to become the open AI-infrastructure for real-time Interactive Video & world models.
- [Livepeer Evolution](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/home/about-livepeer/evolution): A look at the Livepeer evolution from video to AI, to participatory governance and community driven development.
- [The Livepeer Advantage](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/home/about-livepeer/benefits): Livepeer offers an open, cost-effective, performant, and scalable trusted execution layer for the interactive AI-first internet.
- [Livepeer Organisational Structure](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/home/about-livepeer/ecosystem): A look at the Livepeer organisational structure, including the Livepeer Foundation, Livepeer Inc., and the key role of the Livepeer Community in governing the future of network.
- [What's Next for Livepeer?](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/home/about-livepeer/roadmap): What's next for Livepeer? A look at the Livepeer Foundation Roadmap - get involved!

### Home / Showcase

- [Creative Projects Built with Livepeer](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/home/solutions/showcase): Be inspired by this collection of creative ecosystem use cases re-inventing their industries with Livepeer.
- [Industry Verticals](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/home/solutions/verticals): Explore how Livepeer infrastructure supports entertainment, gaming, robotics, analytics, and other real-time AI video verticals.
- [Key Applications for Livepeer](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/home/solutions/applications): Key Applications of Livepeer from the hobby developer to the Fortune 500.


## About


## Solutions

### Solutions / Use Livepeer

- [Livepeer Solutions Providers](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/portal): Welcome To The Solutions Portal: Explore, Build, Create
- [Solution Providers](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/solution-providers): Ecosystem solution providers and products building on Livepeer.

### Solutions / Daydream

- [About Daydream](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/daydream/overview): Open-source toolkit for world models and real-time AI video on the Livepeer network.
- [Daydream Recent Announcements](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/daydream/community): Latest videos, socials, and community activity for Daydream.
- [Daydream Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/daydream/changelog): Release history for Daydream — open-source real-time AI video tools on Livepeer.

### Solutions / Livepeer Studio

- [About Livepeer Studio](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/overview): Developer platform for live streaming and video-on-demand on the Livepeer network.
- [Livepeer Studio Recent Announcements](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/community): Latest videos, blog posts, and community activity for Livepeer Studio.
- [Livepeer Studio Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/changelog): Release history for Livepeer Studio — video infrastructure APIs and dashboard.

### Solutions / Stream.place

- [About Stream.Place](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/streamplace/overview): Open-source, decentralised video layer for social networks and Web3 apps, built on Livepeer.
- [Streamplace Recent Announcements](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/streamplace/community): Latest community activity, Discord announcements, and GitHub updates for Streamplace.
- [Streamplace Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/streamplace/changelog): Release history for Streamplace — open-source decentralised video layer on Livepeer.

### Solutions / Embody Avatars

- [About Embody Avatars and Agents](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/embody/overview): Real-time interactive 3D avatars for education, training, and communication, powered by Livepeer.
- [Embody Recent Announcements](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/embody/community): Latest community activity, Discord announcements, and GitHub updates for Embody.
- [Embody Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/embody/changelog): Release history for Embody — real-time AI avatars powered by Livepeer.

### Solutions / Frameworks

- [About Frameworks](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/frameworks/overview): Full-stack video infrastructure: self-hosted or hosted, built on MistServer and Livepeer.
- [Frameworks Recent Announcements](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/frameworks/community): Latest community activity, Discord announcements, and GitHub updates for Frameworks.
- [Frameworks Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/frameworks/changelog): Release history for Frameworks — sovereign video infrastructure on Livepeer.

### Solutions / Resources / Compendium

- [Solutions Glossary](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/resources/compendium/glossary): Key terms from the Livepeer Solutions section — use cases, product offerings, AI pipelines, video infrastructure, and integration patterns.

### Livepeer Studio Docs / Get started

- [Getting Started with Livepeer Studio](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/get-started/overview): Learn how to get started with Livepeer Studio
- [Quickstart](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/quickstart): Create an account, get an API key, and add live or on-demand video in under 5 minutes.
- [Authentication](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/get-started/authentication): Learn how to authenticate requests to the Livepeer Studio API
- [Studio CLI](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/get-started/studio-cli): Scaffold a new Livepeer app with the Livepeer Studio CLI.

### Livepeer Studio Docs / Livestream

- [Livestream overview](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/livestream/overview): Concepts for livestreams in Livepeer Studio: streams, sessions, ingest, playback, and health.
- [Create a livestream](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/livestream/create-livestream): Create a livestream with the Livepeer Studio API and get a stream key and playback ID.
- [Play a livestream](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/livestream/playback-livestream): Play livestreams in your app with the Livepeer Player or a custom HLS/WebRTC player.
- [Stream via OBS](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/livestream/stream-via-obs): Configure OBS Studio to stream into Livepeer Studio using RTMP and the stream key.
- [Livestream from browser](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/livestream/livestream-from-browser): Broadcast live from the browser using WebRTC and the Livepeer Broadcast component or custom WHIP.
- [Multistream](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/livestream/multistream): Send one livestream to multiple RTMP/RTMPS destinations (e.g. Twitch, YouTube).
- [Clip a livestream](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/livestream/clip-livestream): Create short clips from active livestreams using the Create Clip API.
- [Stream health](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/livestream/stream-health): Monitor livestream health: transcoding, real-time status, multistream targets, ingest rate, and logs.
- [Optimize latency](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/livestream/optimize-latency): Achieve low or ultra-low latency with WebRTC and HLS and tune OBS and in-browser broadcast settings.

### Livepeer Studio Docs / Video on demand

- [Video on demand overview](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/video-on-demand/overview): Concepts for VOD in Livepeer Studio: assets, playback, recordings, clips, and encrypted assets.
- [Upload an asset](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/video-on-demand/upload-asset): Upload video files to Livepeer Studio with TUS resumable uploads or PUT.
- [Play an asset](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/video-on-demand/playback-asset): Play VOD assets with the Livepeer Player or a custom HLS/MP4 player.
- [Encrypted assets](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/video-on-demand/encrypted-assets): Upload and play encrypted video with Livepeer Studio and control access.
- [Thumbnails (VOD)](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/video-on-demand/thumbnails-vod): Get thumbnails for video assets from the playback info API (WebVTT and keyframe images).
- [Transcode video](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/video-on-demand/transcode-video): Use the Transcode API to transcode video from and to S3-compatible storage (e.g. Storj, W3S).

### Livepeer Studio Docs / Access control & security

- [Access control overview](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/access-control/overview): Restrict playback for streams and assets with webhooks or JWTs.
- [Access control with webhooks](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/access-control/webhooks): Gate playback using a webhook that validates the viewer and returns allow or deny.
- [Access control with JWTs](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/access-control/jwt): Gate playback using signed JWTs so only viewers with a valid token can watch.

### Livepeer Studio Docs / Events & analytics

- [Webhooks](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/analytics/webhooks): Receive real-time events from Livepeer Studio (stream, asset, recording, multistream, task, access control).
- [Listen to events](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/analytics/listen-to-events): Use webhooks to react to stream and asset events (started, ready, failed, etc.).
- [Analytics](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/analytics/overview): Viewership and engagement metrics via the Livepeer API, Grafana, and Timeplus.

### Livepeer Studio Docs / Player & embed

- [Player and embed](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/player): Use the Livepeer Player component and the lvpr.tv embeddable player for playback.

### Livepeer Studio Docs / Reference

- [API overview](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/reference/api): Livepeer Studio REST API: authentication and basic usage.
- [API Reference](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/reference/overview): Complete API reference for Livepeer Studio endpoints
- [SDKs overview](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/reference/sdks): Server and client SDKs for the Livepeer Studio API: TypeScript, Go, Python, and React.
- [Managing projects](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/reference/managing-projects): Organize streams, assets, and API keys with Livepeer Studio projects (staging, production, multiple apps).

### Livepeer Studio Docs / API Reference / Assets

- [Assets API](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/assets/overview): The Assets API is used to create, retrieve, update, delete assets object from pipeline
- [Upload an asset](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/assets/upload): Upload a video or audio asset
- [Upload Asset via URL](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/assets/upload-via-url): Upload an asset by providing a URL
- [Retrieve an asset](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/assets/get): Get details about a specific asset
- [Update Asset](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/assets/update): Update an asset
- [Delete Asset](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/assets/delete): Delete an asset
- [Get All Assets](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/assets/get-all): Retrieve all assets

### Livepeer Studio Docs / API Reference / Streams

- [Streams API](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/streams/overview): The Livestream API is used to create, retrieve, update, delete stream object from pipeline
- [Create a livestream](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/streams/create): Create a new livestream
- [Retrieve a livestream](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/streams/get): Get details about a specific livestream
- [Get All Streams](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/streams/get-all): Retrieve all livestreams
- [Update Stream](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/streams/update): Update a livestream
- [Terminate Stream](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/streams/terminate): Terminate an active livestream
- [Create Clip](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/streams/create-clip): Create a clip from a livestream
- [Get Clip](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/streams/get-clip): Retrieve a clip from a livestream
- [Add Multistream Target](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/streams/add-multistream-target): Add a multistreaming target to a livestream
- [Delete Multistream Target](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/streams/delete-multistream-target): Remove a multistreaming target from a livestream
- [Delete Stream](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/streams/delete): Delete a livestream

### Livepeer Studio Docs / API Reference / Multistream

- [Multistream API](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/multistream/overview): The Multistream API is used to manage multistreaming targets
- [Create Multistream Target](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/multistream/create): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Get Multistream Target](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/multistream/get): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Get All Multistream Targets](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/multistream/get-all): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Update Multistream Target](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/multistream/update): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Delete Multistream Target](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/multistream/delete): Livepeer Studio API endpoint

### Livepeer Studio Docs / API Reference / Playback

- [Playback API](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/playback/overview): The Playback API is used to retrieve playback object from pipeline
- [Retrieve Playback Info](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/playback/get): Get playback information for a stream or asset

### Livepeer Studio Docs / API Reference / Sessions

- [Sessions API](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/sessions/overview): The Sessions API is used to retrieve session information
- [Get Session](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/sessions/get): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Get All Sessions](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/sessions/get-all): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Get Session Clip](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/sessions/get-clip): Livepeer Studio API endpoint

### Livepeer Studio Docs / API Reference / Tasks

- [Tasks API](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/tasks/overview): The Tasks API is used to monitor task status
- [Get Task](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/tasks/get): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Get All Tasks](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/tasks/get-all): Livepeer Studio API endpoint

### Livepeer Studio Docs / API Reference / Transcode

- [Transcode API](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/transcode/overview): The Transcode API is used for on-demand video transcoding
- [Create Transcode Job](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/transcode/create): Livepeer Studio API endpoint

### Livepeer Studio Docs / API Reference / Signing Keys

- [Signing Keys API](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/signing-keys/overview): The Signing Keys API is used to manage signing keys for secure playback
- [Create Signing Key](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/signing-keys/create): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Get Signing Key](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/signing-keys/get): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Get All Signing Keys](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/signing-keys/get-all): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Update Signing Key](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/signing-keys/update): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Delete Signing Key](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/signing-keys/delete): Livepeer Studio API endpoint

### Livepeer Studio Docs / API Reference / Webhooks

- [Webhooks API](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/webhooks/overview): The Webhooks API is used to manage webhook configurations
- [Create Webhook](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/webhooks/create): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Get Webhook](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/webhooks/get): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Get All Webhooks](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/webhooks/get-all): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Update Webhook](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/webhooks/update): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Delete Webhook](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/webhooks/delete): Livepeer Studio API endpoint

### Livepeer Studio Docs / API Reference / Rooms

- [Rooms API](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/rooms/overview): The Rooms API is used to manage WebRTC rooms
- [Create Room](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/rooms/create): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Get Room](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/rooms/get): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Delete Room](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/rooms/delete): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Create Room User](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/rooms/create-user): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Get Room User](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/rooms/get-user): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Update Room User](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/rooms/update-user): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Remove Room User](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/rooms/remove-user): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Start Room Egress](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/rooms/start-egress): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Stop Room Egress](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/rooms/stop-egress): Livepeer Studio API endpoint

### Livepeer Studio Docs / API Reference / Viewership

- [Viewership API](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/viewership/overview): The Viewership API provides analytics and metrics
- [Get Usage Metrics](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/viewership/get-usage-metrics): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Get Viewership Metrics](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/viewership/get-viewership-metrics): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Get Creators Metrics](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/viewership/get-creators-metrics): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Get Public Total Views](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/viewership/get-public-total-views): Livepeer Studio API endpoint
- [Get Realtime Viewership](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/solutions/livepeer-studio/docs/api-reference/viewership/get-realtime-viewership): Livepeer Studio API endpoint


## Developers

### Developers / Hub

- [Livepeer Developer Portal](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/portal): Welcome To The Developer Portal: Explore, Build, Create
- [Developer Journey](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/developer-journey): Find your path on Livepeer - whether you want to build video apps, run AI pipelines, operate a gateway, or contribute to the protocol. Start here.

### Developers / Concepts

- [The Livepeer Developer Landscape](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/concepts/developer-stack): Explains the relationship between Studio, Daydream, the AI gateway API, ComfyStream, and the raw Livepeer protocol — so you can choose the right layer to build at.
- [AI on Livepeer](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/concepts/ai-on-livepeer): Explains the three AI pipeline categories on the Livepeer network — batch inference, real-time AI (live-video-to-video), and LLM — so you can choose the right path for your workload.
- [Video on Livepeer](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/concepts/video-on-livepeer): Explains video streaming and transcoding on the Livepeer network for developers.
- [When to Run Your Own Gateway](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/concepts/running-a-gateway): A decision guide for developers considering self-hosting a Livepeer gateway — covering the trade-offs between hosted APIs and self-hosted infrastructure, and what self-hosting requires.
- [The Livepeer Open Source Stack](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/concepts/oss-stack): Maps the main Livepeer open-source repositories — go-livepeer, livepeer-protocol, ai-worker, comfystream, and livepeer.js — and explains how they connect, for developers wanting to contribute.

### Developers / Get Started

- [How to Get Started](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/get-started/setup-paths): Choose your developer path and get to your first successful Livepeer integration.
- [Quickstart: Video Streaming](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/get-started/video-quickstart): Stream and transcode video using the Livepeer Studio API.
- [Transcoding Jobs Quickstart](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/get-started/transcoding-quickstart): Submit a Livepeer Studio Transcode job, poll the task status, and verify completion. Built from the GitHub-validated Studio OpenAPI spec and pending TD accuracy review.
- [AI Jobs Quickstart](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/get-started/ai-quickstart): Send your first AI job to a Livepeer gateway using the AI API, with GitHub-validated endpoint references and a stakeholder signoff checklist.
- [Quickstart: ComfyStream on Livepeer](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/get-started/comfystream-quickstart): Get a ComfyStream instance running and connected to the Livepeer network in a single session — covering Docker, RunPod, and local install paths.

### Developers / Build

- [Build with ComfyStream](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/build/comfystream): Reference for ComfyStream pipeline modes, custom nodes, workflow format, data-channel output, and performance tuning for developers building real-time AI video applications on Livepeer.
- [Bring Your Own Container (BYOC)](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/build/byoc): Step-by-step guide to building a custom AI container using the PyTrickle integration layer and deploying it as a worker on the Livepeer network.
- [Build a Gateway Client with the SDK](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/build/sdk-gateway): Build a lightweight Livepeer gateway client in Python, JavaScript, or mobile using the SDK and remote signer — no Go required.
- [Is My AI Workload a Good Fit for Livepeer?](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/build/workload-fit): Decision framework for evaluating whether your AI workload belongs on Livepeer. Includes decision tree, capability matrix, gateway vs orchestrator responsibilities, and anti-patterns.
- [AI Model Support](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/build/model-support): Reference tables for all AI pipeline types supported on the Livepeer network, with supported model architectures, minimum GPU VRAM requirements, and current status.

### Developers / Guides

- [Developer Guides](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/guides/developer-guides): A curated library of how-to guides, tutorials, and walkthroughs for building with Livepeer — from first API call to production AI pipelines.
- [Resources](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/guides/resources): A curated library of tools, dashboards, reading, videos, and reference material from across the Livepeer ecosystem.
- [Contribution Guide](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/guides/contribution-guide): How to contribute to Livepeer — code, documentation, governance, community support, and bug reports across all repositories.
- [Developer Help](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/guides/developer-help): Every way to get help as a Livepeer developer — Discord channels, forum, office hours, GitHub issues, and more.
- [Contract Addresses](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/guides/testnet-contract-deployment): Current and historical Livepeer protocol contract addresses across Arbitrum One and Ethereum Mainnet. Verified on-chain.
- [Deploy a local testnet](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/guides/local-testnet-deployment): Deploy the full Livepeer protocol stack locally using Hardhat and connect go-livepeer nodes to your own contracts for development and testing.

### Developers / Opportunities

- [Builder Opportunities](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/opportunities/overview): Find grants, programmes, RFPs, treasury proposals, open source contribution pathways, and bug bounties for every type of Livepeer builder.
- [Grants & Programmes](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/opportunities/grants-and-programmes): Livepeer funding opportunities for app builders, AI workflow creators, orchestrators, and research teams -- from microgrants to multi-month accelerator programmes.
- [RFPs & Treasury Proposals](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/opportunities/rfps-and-proposals): Apply for Foundation-issued Requests for Proposals (RFPs) or submit a funded Special Purpose Entity (SPE) proposal to the Livepeer onchain treasury.
- [Open Source Contributions](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/opportunities/oss-contributions): How to contribute to Livepeer's open source repositories -- from documentation fixes to protocol engineering -- including a guide to every core repo and its contributing process.
- [Bug Bounties](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/opportunities/bug-bounties): Report smart contract vulnerabilities in the Livepeer protocol and earn USDC rewards through the official Livepeer bug bounty programme on Immunefi.

### Developers / Resources / Compendium

- [Developer Glossary](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/resources/compendium/glossary): Key terms for Livepeer developers — SDKs, APIs, AI pipelines, streaming protocols, and integration concepts.

### Developers / Resources

- [SDKs](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/resources/sdks): SDKs for Livepeer - Studio SDKs and Network SDKs
- [APIs](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/resources/apis): APIs for Livepeer - Studio API and Network APIs
- [Awesome Livepeer](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/resources/awesome-livepeer): Browse the community-curated Awesome Livepeer collection of tools, libraries, examples, and ecosystem resources.
- [Livepeer Wiki](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/resources/wiki): Access the Livepeer Wiki README and repository for older technical reference material and project documentation.
- [DeepWiki](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/developers/resources/deepwiki): Use DeepWiki to explore Livepeer repositories, architecture, and workflows through AI-generated codebase documentation and search.


## Gateways


## Orchestrators

### Orchestrators / Start Here

- [Orchestrator Portal](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/portal): Welcome To The Orchestrator Portal: Explore, Earn, Operate
- [Find Your Path](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/navigator): Choose the right orchestrator path based on your situation - hardware, stake, workload goals, and operational model.

### Orchestrators / Concepts

- [The Orchestrator Role in the Livepeer Network](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/concepts/role): What orchestrators are, how they fit into the Livepeer compute network, and how the role has evolved from video transcoding to AI inference.
- [Orchestrator Capabilities](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/concepts/capabilities): What Orchestrators can execute, the workload types they support across video and AI, how Gateways select them, and the boundaries of the Orchestrator role.
- [Orchestrator Architecture](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/concepts/architecture): How Orchestrators fit into the Livepeer stack - layer position, internal components, request flow, GPU worker management, and protocol interactions.
- [Orchestrator Incentive Model](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/concepts/incentive-model): How Orchestrators earn - the two revenue streams, cost structure, payment flow, pricing configuration, and what the earnings case looks like in practice.

### Orchestrators / Quickstart

- [Orchestrator Quickstart](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/quickstart/guide): Choose your quickstart path — video transcoding, AI inference, or join a pool — and get your first orchestrator node running.
- [Video Transcoding Quickstart](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/quickstart/video-transcoding): Run go-livepeer as a transcoding orchestrator: create your wallet, configure your node, register, and verify.
- [Quickstart Tutorial](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/quickstart/tutorial): Follow the quickest guided path to verify Livepeer video or AI workloads on your hardware before moving to production setup.
- [Add AI to Your Node](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/quickstart/AI-prompt-start): An Experimental AI Prompt for setting up your GPU as a Livepeer Orchestrator. Add AI inference pipelines to an existing go-livepeer transcoding node — hardware check, aiModels.json configuration, a...

### Orchestrators / Setup

- [Setup Overview](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/setup/guide): Default setup path for Livepeer orchestrators running go-livepeer, with redirects for pools and non-default deployment patterns.
- [Operational and Hardware Requirements for Orchestrators](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/setup/rcs-requirements): Minimum, recommended, and production hardware for running a Livepeer orchestrator.
- [Install go-livepeer](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/setup/rs-install): Install the go-livepeer binary on Linux, macOS, or via Docker. Covers prerequisites, GPU driver requirements, installation steps, and verification.
- [Configuring Your Orchestrator](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/setup/configure): Set the go-livepeer flags needed to run as an Orchestrator - GPU selection, pricing, session limits, networking, and AI worker configuration.
- [Connect to Arbitrum](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/setup/connect-and-activate): Connect the orchestrator to the Livepeer protocol on Arbitrum - fund the wallet, stake LPT, register the service URI, and activate on-chain.
- [Verify Your Setup](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/setup/test): Verify the orchestrator is on-chain, processing jobs, and healthy - video transcoding test, AI inference test, Explorer check, and basic monitoring confirmation.
- [Monitoring Your Orchestrator Setup](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/setup/r-monitor): Monitor node health, GPU performance, and earnings with Prometheus and the Livepeer Explorer.

### Orchestrators / Guides / Operator Considerations

- [Operator Rationale](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/operator-considerations/operator-rationale): title: 'Operating Rationale' sidebarTitle: Operator Rationale description: >- Cost categories, revenue sources, viability thresholds, and a decision matrix for evaluating whether running a Livepeer...
- [The Commercial Case](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/operator-considerations/business-case): How commercial Orchestrators operate - earning from service fees rather than inflation, building Gateway relationships, and positioning for application workloads.
- [Operator Impact](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/operator-considerations/operator-impact): How Orchestrators shape the Livepeer protocol - governance weight, what gets voted on, the sovereign compute thesis, and why open video infrastructure matters.
- [Requirements](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/operator-considerations/requirements): GPU support policy, NVENC session limits by card tier, VRAM requirements by AI pipeline, system stack minimums, capacity testing with livepeer_bench, and a pre-launch readiness checklist.

### Orchestrators / Guides / Deployment Details

- [Setup Options](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/deployment-details/setup-options): A guide to Livepeer GPU setup options - including installing with go-livepeer, using Siphon, joining a pool or operating concurrent workloads.
- [Siphon Split Setup](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/deployment-details/siphon-setup): Separate reward claiming and keystore management from GPU workload processing using OrchestratorSiphon - two-machine architecture, installation, config.ini setup, systemd service, and day-to-day op...
- [Dual Mode Configuration](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/deployment-details/dual-mode-configuration): Configure your orchestrator node to serve both video transcoding and AI inference jobs from a single go-livepeer process.
- [Orchestrator-Transcoder Split Setup](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/deployment-details/orchestrator-transcoder-setup): Run go-livepeer's Orchestrator and Transcoder as separate processes on separate machines - isolating protocol operations from GPU workload, scaling Transcoders independently, and connecting multipl...
- [Join a Pool](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/deployment-details/join-a-pool): Contribute GPU capacity to an existing Livepeer orchestrator and earn rewards without running protocol infrastructure.
- [Join a Pool](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/deployment-details/new-join-a-pool): How to contribute GPU compute to an existing Livepeer Orchestrator pool - what pools are, how to evaluate one, how to connect as a worker, and what to expect from payouts.

### Orchestrators / Guides / Workloads and AI

- [Workload Options](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/ai-and-job-workloads/workload-options): The four job types an orchestrator can process — transcoding, batch AI inference, Cascade live-video AI, and LLM inference — with hardware requirements, pricing models, routing mechanics, and how t...
- [Video Transcoding Operations](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/ai-and-job-workloads/video-transcoding-operations): Configure video transcoding on your Livepeer orchestrator — wei and USD pricing, automatic price adjustment, session limits, bandwidth calculation, NVENC session caps, and output rendition profiles.
- [AI Inference Operations](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/ai-and-job-workloads/ai-inference-operations): How AI inference works on the Livepeer network — pipeline types, the batch vs live-video distinction, hardware requirements, and how jobs flow from application to your GPU node.
- [Diffusion Pipeline Setup](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/ai-and-job-workloads/diffusion-pipeline-setup): Complete operator guide for running batch AI inference pipelines on a Livepeer orchestrator — aiModels.json configuration, all supported pipeline types, Ollama LLM runner deployment, BYOC external...
- [LLM Pipeline Setup](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/ai-and-job-workloads/llm-pipeline-setup): Set up the llm pipeline on a Livepeer orchestrator using the Ollama-based runner. Covers Docker Compose configuration, model selection for 8 GB VRAM GPUs, model ID mapping, aiModels.json entries, a...
- [Cascade Setup](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/ai-and-job-workloads/realtime-ai-setup): Deploy the live-video-to-video pipeline on a Livepeer orchestrator — Cascade architecture, ComfyStream setup, live-video frame processing, WebRTC streaming, hardware requirements, and custom pipeli...
- [Audio and Vision Pipelines](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/ai-and-job-workloads/audio-and-vision-pipelines): Set up audio-to-text (Whisper), text-to-speech, image-to-text, and segment-anything-2 pipelines on a Livepeer orchestrator. Covers VRAM requirements, aiModels.json entries, pricing units, and testi...
- [Model and Demand Reference](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/ai-and-job-workloads/model-demand-reference): Operator reference for GPU memory planning on Livepeer — VRAM requirements by pipeline, warm model strategy, multi-GPU configuration, aiModels.json complete schema, pricing reference, and earnings...
- [Model Hosting](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/ai-and-job-workloads/model-hosting): Source, download, and store AI models for Livepeer orchestrator pipelines. Covers HuggingFace model IDs, automatic vs manual download, storage layout, gated model access via tokens, and the Livepee...

### Orchestrators / Guides / Staking and Earning

- [Earning Model](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/staking-and-rewards/earning-model): How orchestrators earn on Livepeer — ETH service fees, LPT inflationary rewards, commission parameters, what wins jobs, and how AI changes the fee landscape.
- [Reward Mechanics](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/staking-and-rewards/reward-mechanics): Protocol reward mechanics for Livepeer orchestrators — how LPT inflation flows, calling Reward() automatically and manually, gas thresholds on Arbitrum, fee redemption, setting commission, and moni...
- [Payment Receipts](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/payments-and-pricing/payment-receipts): How probabilistic micropayment tickets arrive, when they win, how the Redeemer submits them on-chain, and how to verify ticket redemption is working correctly.
- [Payments](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/payments-and-pricing/payments): How ETH moves from gateway to your orchestrator wallet — probabilistic micropayments, winning ticket mechanics, the Redeemer, on-chain redemption on Arbitrum, and keeping your node funded for trans...
- [Delegate Operations](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/staking-and-rewards/delegate-operations): How to attract and retain LPT delegators as a Livepeer orchestrator — what delegators evaluate, the Explorer ROI formulas explained, commission strategy, reputation, and the compounding stake flywh...
- [Network Participation](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/staking-and-rewards/network-participation): How Livepeer protocol governance works and how orchestrators vote on Livepeer Improvement Proposals (LIPs) — finding polls, voting via livepeer_cli, verifying your vote on Explorer, and where to fo...

### Orchestrators / Guides / Config and Optimisation

- [Pricing Strategy](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/config-and-optimisation/pricing-strategy): Set competitive pricing for video and AI workloads on a Livepeer orchestrator. Covers pricePerUnit, per-pipeline AI pricing in aiModels.json, pricePerGateway, autoAdjustPrice, and market positionin...
- [Capacity Planning](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/config-and-optimisation/capacity-planning): Determine the correct -maxSessions value for your orchestrator using livepeer_bench, bandwidth calculations, and NVENC hardware session limits. Covers GPU and CPU transcoding capacity, AI VRAM budg...
- [AI Model Management](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/config-and-optimisation/ai-model-management): Manage warm and cold model strategy, VRAM allocation, model rotation by demand, and optimisation flags for diffusion pipelines on a Livepeer orchestrator.
- [Reward Call Tuning](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/config-and-optimisation/reward-call-tuning): Calculate whether reward calling is profitable at your stake level, configure automatic vs manual calling, and optimise timing to minimise gas costs on Arbitrum.

### Orchestrators / Guides / Monitoring and Tools

- [Operator Toolbox](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/monitoring-and-tooling/operator-toolbox): Every tool for monitoring, verifying, and operating your Livepeer orchestrator — Explorer, Prometheus metrics, Docker monitoring stack, community dashboards, and capability testing utilities.
- [Explorer Operations](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/monitoring-and-tooling/explorer-operations): A walkthrough of every metric on your Livepeer Explorer orchestrator page — what each number means, what healthy looks like, and what to do when something is wrong.
- [Metrics and Alerting](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/monitoring-and-tooling/metrics-and-alerting): How to set up Prometheus, Grafana, and the Docker monitoring stack for your Livepeer orchestrator. Covers the -monitor flag, key metrics, AI runner container monitoring, and log capture.
- [Troubleshooting](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/monitoring-and-tooling/troubleshooting): Diagnostic guide for common Livepeer orchestrator errors — transcoding failures, GPU issues, reward calling problems, AI runner errors, networking issues, and account errors. With causes and action...

### Orchestrators / Guides / Advanced Operations

- [Gateway Relationships](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/advanced-operations/gateway-relationships): How gateways discover and select your orchestrator — the discovery mechanisms, multi-factor selection algorithm, what you can do to receive more work, and how to use the gateway Loki API to underst...
- [Gateway and Orchestrator Interface](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/advanced-operations/gateway-orchestrator-interface): Configure a node running both a gateway and an orchestrator on Livepeer. Covers deployment architecture, port allocation, self-routing behaviour, pricing alignment, and the off-chain gateway with o...
- [Pool Operators](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/advanced-operations/pool-operators): How to operate a Livepeer GPU pool — accept external worker connections, manage job routing across multiple GPUs, handle off-chain fee distribution, and maintain operational transparency.
- [Scale Operations](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/advanced-operations/scale-operations): Architectural patterns for running multiple Livepeer orchestrator nodes at data-centre scale — multi-orchestrator setup, capacity management, rolling updates, fleet-wide monitoring, and enterprise...

### Orchestrators / Guides / Roadmap and Funding

- [Funding and Support](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/roadmap-and-funding/funding-and-support): Funding programmes, grants, and community resources for Livepeer orchestrator operators - SPE grants, AI Video Startup Programme.
- [Orchestrator Profiles](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/roadmap-and-funding/orchestrator-profiles): Active orchestrator operators on the Livepeer network - who is operating, what they have built, and community highlights.

### Orchestrators / Guides / Tutorials

- [BYOC CPU Smoke Test](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/tutorials/byoc-cpu-smoke-test): Verify the Livepeer orchestrator framework works on your machine in under 20 minutes - no GPU, no wallet, no on-chain registration required.
- [Zero to First Reward](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/tutorials/zero-to-first-reward): End-to-end tutorial: install go-livepeer, configure video transcoding, fund and stake LPT, activate on the Livepeer network, and claim a first LPT reward.
- [AI Earning Quickstart](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/tutorials/ai-earning-quickstart): Start earning from AI inference in under 2 hours - one GPU, one warm model, no active set membership required.
- [Add AI to a Video Node](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/tutorials/add-ai-to-video-node): Add AI inference to an existing video orchestrator. Covers the exact configuration delta, VRAM headroom check, and dual earnings verification.
- [Full AI Pipeline Tutorial](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/tutorials/full-ai-pipeline-tutorial): End-to-end tutorial: gateway routes an inference request to an orchestrator, the AI runner processes it, and the result returns through the full pipeline. Covers off-chain local setup with a Huggin...
- [Realtime AI Tutorial](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/guides/tutorials/realtime-ai-tutorial): Set up live video-to-video AI processing using ComfyStream and the Cascade pipeline. A video stream enters, a transformed stream exits in under 100ms per frame.

### Orchestrators / Resources / Reference / Technical Reference

- [CLI Flags Reference](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/resources/reference/technical/cli-flags): CLI flags and gRPC field mapping for go-livepeer orchestrators.
- [Canonical Contract Addresses](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/resources/reference/technical/contract-addresses): Official Livepeer protocol contract addresses on Arbitrum One and Ethereum Mainnet. Verified on-chain and automatically updated from the canonical governor-scripts source. The only official referen...
- [GPU Support Matrix](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/resources/reference/gpu-support): NVIDIA GPU compatibility, NVENC/NVDEC session limits, driver requirements, and CUDA versions for Livepeer orchestrators.
- [Arbitrum RPCs](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/resources/reference/arbitrum-rpc): Arbitrum One RPC endpoint options for Livepeer orchestrators — provider comparison, rate limits, and configuration.
- [Arbitrum Exchanges](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/resources/reference/arbitrum-exchanges): Where to acquire ETH (for gas) and LPT (for staking) on Arbitrum One — exchanges, bridges, and DEXs for Livepeer orchestrators.

### Orchestrators / Resources / Reference / Compendium

- [Orchestrator Glossary](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/resources/reference/compendium/glossary): Key terms for Livepeer orchestrator operators — GPU setup, AI pipelines, staking, payment mechanics, monitoring, and protocol roles.

### Orchestrators / Resources / Reference

- [FAQ and Troubleshooting](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/resources/reference/faq): Answers to common orchestrator questions and step-by-step fixes for the most frequent errors — installation, networking, job routing, AI pipelines, and earnings.
- [Orchestrator Terminology Glossary](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/resources/reference/glossary): Terminology and concept definitions for Livepeer orchestrators - deployment axes, node modes, staking terms, and key infrastructure concepts.

### Orchestrators / Resources / Knowledge Hub

- [Community Guides & Tutorials](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/resources/knowledge-hub/community-guides): Curated external tutorials, video walkthroughs, and community-maintained resources for Livepeer orchestrators — from setup through advanced operations.
- [Community Orchestrator Pools](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/orchestrators/resources/knowledge-hub/community-pools): Compare community orchestrator pools, how they work, and what to review before joining one.


## Delegators

### Delegators / About LPT

- [Delegators Home Portal](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/token-portal): Welcome to the Delegators Portal: explore, navigate, delegate, and vote
- [LPT Overview](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/about/overview): Comprehensive architectural and economic overview of the Livepeer Token (LPT) within the Livepeer Protocol.
- [Livepeer Token Purpose](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/about/purpose): Core purpose of LPT in Livepeer: protocol security collateral, reward distribution, and governance participation.
- [LPT Tokenomics](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/about/tokenomics): Formal economic model of LPT issuance, bonding dynamics, reward distribution, and security equilibrium.
- [LPT Mechanics](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/about/mechanics): Detailed protocol-level mechanics governing bonding, unbonding, reward checkpointing, and round-based state transitions in the Livepeer Protocol.

### Delegators / Delegating LPT

- [Delegating LPT: Getting Started](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/delegation/getting-started): How to delegate LPT to an orchestrator, earn rewards, and participate in Livepeer network security - a step-by-step guide for new delegators.
- [Delegation Overview](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/delegation/overview): Protocol-level overview of LPT delegation, including stake attribution, reward flows, and security implications.
- [Delegation Economics](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/delegation/delegation-economics): Interim overview of delegation economics and stake-weighted incentives for LPT delegators and orchestrators.
- [About Delegators](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/delegation/about-delegators): Formal definition, incentive role, rights/constraints, and risk model for delegators in the Livepeer Protocol.
- [Delegation Guide](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/delegation/delegation-guide): Contract-level, step-by-step guide to bonding, delegating, unbonding, rebonding, and verifying LPT delegation on-chain.
- [Bridge LPT between Ethereum and Arbitrum](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/delegation/bridge-lpt-to-arbitrum): Move LPT to Arbitrum One to stake, delegate, and participate in Livepeer governance — via bridge, CEX, or DEX swap.

### Delegators / Livepeer Governance

- [Livepeer Governance Overview](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/governance/overview): Architectural and economic overview of stake-weighted governance in the Livepeer Protocol.
- [Livepeer Governance Model](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/governance/model): Formal model of stake-weighted governance, quorum mechanics, voting thresholds, timelock execution, and attack surface analysis.
- [Livepeer Governance Processes](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/governance/processes): End-to-end governance process lifecycle including off-chain signaling, LIP drafting, on-chain proposal execution, and treasury coordination.

### Delegators / Livepeer Treasury

- [Treasury Overview](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/treasury/overview): Formal model of the Livepeer Treasury as a governance-controlled protocol asset allocator.
- [Treasury Proposals](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/treasury/proposals): Formal structure and execution semantics of treasury proposals as governance-authorized on-chain actions.
- [Treasury Allocations](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/treasury/allocations): Allocation design, accounting model, evaluation framework, and verification/audit approach for governance-authorized treasury spending.

### Delegators / Guides & Resources / Compendium

- [LPT Token Glossary](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/resources/compendium/glossary): Key terms for the Livepeer Token (LPT) — staking, delegation, inflation, governance, treasury, and tokenomics.

### Delegators / Guides & Resources

- [Delegator Videos and Blogs](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/resources/delegator-videos-and-blogs): Curated video and blog resources for delegators: staking tutorials, LPT delegation guides, and official links.
- [Exchanges with LPT Listed](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/resources/exchanges): A list of exchanges where the Livepeer Token (LPT) is listed. Note: this information is fetched from Coingecko weekly
- [LPT & ETH Token Functions](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/delegators/resources/lpt-eth-usage): What the LPT & ETH Tokens are used for in the Livepeer Protocol


## Community

### Community / Livepeer Community

- [Livepeer Community Portal](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/community/community-portal): Welcome To The Community Portal: Explore, Connect, Contribute
- [Community Guidelines](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/community/livepeer-community/community-guidelines): How we show up for each other - standards, conduct, and contribution norms for the Livepeer community.
- [Governance & the Livepeer Foundation](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/community/livepeer-community/governance-and-foundation): How Livepeer is governed, who the Livepeer Foundation is, how the treasury works, and how to participate in decisions that shape the network.
- [Trending Topics](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/community/livepeer-community/trending-topics): Trending Topics at Livepeer from across the forum, blogs and community.
- [What's Happening in Livepeer](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/community/livepeer-community/livepeer-latest-topics): Curated ecosystem highlights and a live feed of the latest topics from the Livepeer Forum.
- [Livepeer Foundation Roadmap](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/community/livepeer-community/roadmap): What's next for Livepeer? A look at the Livepeer Foundation Roadmap - get involved!

### Community / Livepeer Connect

- [Connect & Channels](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/community/livepeer-connect/forums-and-discussions): Where to find the Livepeer community - forums, Discord, Telegram, social media, and recurring community calls.
- [News & Socials](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/community/livepeer-connect/news-and-socials): Stay up to date with the latest news and socials from the Livepeer Community
- [Events & Live Streams](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/community/livepeer-connect/events-and-community-streams): Upcoming and past Livepeer community events and streams from the shared Livepeer calendar.

### Community / Livepeer Contribute

- [Contribute to Livepeer](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/community/livepeer-contribute/contribute): Help us build the future of open realtime AI & video - it takes a village!
- [Livepeer Opportunities](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/community/livepeer-contribute/opportunities): Current opportunities to participate in Livepeer through grants, programs, and community initiatives.
- [Build Livepeer](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/community/livepeer-contribute/build-livepeer): Ways builders can contribute code, tooling, and ecosystem projects to Livepeer.

### Community / Resources / Compendium

- [Community Glossary](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/community/resources/compendium/glossary): Key terms for the Livepeer community — governance, treasury, grants, delegation, voting, and ecosystem participation.

### Community / Resources

- [Ecosystem Tools & Projects](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/community/resources/awesome-livepeer): A curated overview of community-built tools, dashboards, SDKs, and projects in the Livepeer ecosystem. The full list lives in the awesome-livepeer repository.
- [Community Guides](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/community/resources/guides): Guides, tutorials, and educational resources from the Livepeer community - covering delegation, orchestrator operation, gateway setup, and governance participation.
- [Dashboards](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/community/resources/dashboards): Community dashboards and analytics tools that help monitor ecosystem activity.

### Community / Help Center

- [Community FAQ](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/community/faq): Frequently asked questions about the Livepeer community, governance, ecosystem participation, and getting support.


## Resource HUB

### Resources / Documentation Guide / Contributing

- [Contributing to the Livepeer Docs](https://docs.livepeer.org/docs-guide/contributing/contributing): Canonical contributor workflow for docs content, components, tooling, quality gates, and PR process.
- [Contribute To The Docs](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/documentation-guide/contributing/contribute-to-the-docs): Contribute to the Livepeer documentation and help us make it even better!
- [Livepeer Docs Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/changelog/docs): This changelog tracks significant updates to the Livepeer documentation. For protocol changes, see the Livepeer Improvement Proposals (LIPs) and Livepeer Forum.

### Resources / Documentation Guide / Features

- [Features and AI Integrations](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/documentation-guide/features/docs-features-and-ai-integrations): The Livepeer documentation includes several powerful features designed to enhance your experience and make information more accessible.

### Resources / Documentation Guide / AI & Automations

- [AI Features](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/documentation-guide/ai-automations/ai-features): Livepeer Docs includes built-in AI features for connecting AI clients, LLM tooling, and agent workflows directly to the documentation.
- [Automations & Workflows Guide](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/documentation-guide/ai-automations/automations-workflows): Complete guide to all automation scripts, GitHub Actions workflows, n8n automations, and pre-commit hooks in the Livepeer documentation repository
- [Research and Fact Checking](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/documentation-guide/ai-automations/research-and-fact-checking): How docs contributors and maintainers use the experimental research workflow to verify factual claims, compare related pages, and queue follow-up updates.

### Resources / Documentation Guide / Copy & Style

- [Documentation Style Guide](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/documentation-guide/copy-style/style-guide): Style guide, Mintlify gotchas, and best practices for writing Livepeer documentation
- [Authoring Guide](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/documentation-guide/copy-style/authoring-guide): How to write, structure, and publish documentation pages in the Livepeer docs.
- [Documentation Authoring Standard](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/documentation-guide/copy-style/authoring-standard): Complete guide to the standards for authoring documentation in the Livepeer documentation repository

### Resources / Documentation Guide / Component Library

- [Component Library Overview](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/documentation-guide/component-library/overview): Generated overview for the governed snippets/components library.
- [Component Library](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/documentation-guide/component-library/component-library): Generated component-library landing page backed by the component registry.
- [Elements](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/documentation-guide/component-library/elements): Standalone visual atoms — icons, text, links, callouts, math, spacing, images.
- [Wrappers](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/documentation-guide/component-library/wrappers): Containers that hold, group, or spatially arrange other components.
- [Displays](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/documentation-guide/component-library/displays): Renderers for authored content — code, video, diagrams, quotes, response fields.
- [Scaffolding](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/documentation-guide/component-library/scaffolding): One-per-page structural skeleton — heroes, portals, frame-mode overrides.
- [Integrators](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/documentation-guide/component-library/integrators): Fetches, embeds, or binds to external data — feeds, embeds, video, APIs.
- [Config](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/documentation-guide/component-library/config): Non-component configuration objects (e.g. theme, colour maps).

### Resources / Documentation Guide / Tooling

- [Lpd Cli](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/documentation-guide/tooling/lpd-cli)
- [Snippets Folder Inventory](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/documentation-guide/tooling/snippets-inventory): Governance overview for the repo's snippets directory, including the canonical root guide, generated registry, and current folder taxonomy.

### Resources / Documentation Guide / Catalogs

- [Pages Catalog](https://docs.livepeer.org/docs-guide/catalog/pages-catalog): Tree catalog of docs pages included in docs.json navigation, generated from v2 index data.
- [Components Catalog](https://docs.livepeer.org/docs-guide/catalog/components-catalog): Generated catalog of all governed component exports.
- [Scripts Catalog](https://docs.livepeer.org/docs-guide/catalog/scripts-catalog): Generated catalog of all governed repository scripts, grouped by type with pipeline flow diagram.
- [Templates Catalog](https://docs.livepeer.org/docs-guide/catalog/templates-catalog): Aggregate catalog of repository templates
- [Workflows Catalog](https://docs.livepeer.org/docs-guide/catalog/workflows-catalog): Aggregate catalog of repository GitHub workflows
- [UI Templates](https://docs.livepeer.org/docs-guide/catalog/ui-templates): Generated catalog of canonical UI page and block templates, authoring snippets, and source locations.

### Resources / Documentation Guide

- [Documentation Overview](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/documentation-guide/documentation-overview): This page outlines the ethos, objectives & features of the docs, giving you a clear navigational roadmap
- [Guide To Using The Documentation](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/documentation-guide/documentation-guide): This guide will help you navigate and make the most of the Livepeer documentation site.

### Resources / Technical References

- [Canonical Contract Addresses](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/about/resources/reference/livepeer-contract-addresses): Official Livepeer protocol contract addresses on Arbitrum One and Ethereum Mainnet. Verified from live chain state, upstream source provenance, and explorer evidence on every refresh. The canonical...

### Resources / Compendium

- [Resources Glossary](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/compendium/glossary): Key terms from the Livepeer Resources section — protocol references, external tools, research, community resources, and cross-network concepts.

### Resources / Changelogs / Protocol & Network

- [go-livepeer changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/changelog/protocol/go-livepeer): Notable releases and improvements to the Livepeer node software (go-livepeer). Covers new features, updates, and bug fixes that affect gateway operators, orchestrators, and developers.
- [Livepeer Improvement Proposals Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/changelog/protocol/lips): History of Livepeer Improvement Proposals (LIPs), the formal process for proposing and tracking protocol upgrades.
- [NaaP Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/changelog/protocol/naap): Commit history for NaaP (Network as a Platform), the plugin platform for the Livepeer AI Compute Network.
- [Livepeer Subgraph Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/changelog/protocol/subgraph): Release history for the Livepeer Protocol subgraph, which indexes on-chain protocol data for queries and analytics.

### Resources / Changelogs / AI & Compute

- [AI Runner Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/changelog/ai-compute/ai-runner): Release history for the Livepeer AI Runner, the inference runtime for batch and real-time AI pipelines on the network.
- [ComfyStream Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/changelog/ai-compute/comfystream): Release history for ComfyStream, a ComfyUI custom node for running real-time media workflows with AI-powered video and audio processing.
- [pytrickle Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/changelog/ai-compute/pytrickle): Release history for pytrickle, a Python package for trickle ingress and egress using the trickle protocol.

### Resources / Changelogs / Data & Tooling

- [Livepeer Explorer Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/changelog/tooling/explorer): Release history for the Livepeer Explorer, the web interface for staking, orchestrator discovery, and network analytics.
- [Livepeer Data Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/changelog/tooling/livepeer-data): Release history for the Livepeer Data service.
- [Livepeer Python Gateway Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/changelog/tooling/livepeer-python-gateway): Commit history for the Livepeer Python Gateway.

### Resources / Changelogs / APIs & SDKs

- [Livepeer JavaScript SDK Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/changelog/apis-sdks/livepeer-js): Release history for the Livepeer JavaScript SDK.
- [Livepeer AI JavaScript SDK Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/changelog/apis-sdks/livepeer-ai-js): Release history for the Livepeer AI JavaScript/TypeScript SDK.
- [Livepeer Python SDK Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/changelog/apis-sdks/livepeer-python): Release history for the Livepeer Python SDK.
- [Livepeer AI Python SDK Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/changelog/apis-sdks/livepeer-ai-python): Release history for the Livepeer AI Python SDK.
- [Livepeer AI Go SDK Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/changelog/apis-sdks/livepeer-ai-go): Release history for the Livepeer AI Go SDK.

### Resources / Changelogs / Ecosystem

- [Livepeer.org Website Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/changelog/ecosystem/website): Commit history for the Livepeer.org website repository.
- [Awesome Livepeer Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/changelog/ecosystem/awesome-livepeer): Commit history for Awesome Livepeer, a community curated list of projects, tutorials, demos, and resources within the Livepeer ecosystem.

### Resources / Changelogs

- [Changelog](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/changelog/changelog): This changelog tracks significant updates to the Livepeer documentation. For protocol changes, see the Livepeer Improvement Proposals (LIPs) and Livepeer Forum.

### Resources / Help Center

- [Help Center](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/resources/help-center): Get help with Livepeer. Find answers, report issues, and connect with the community.

### Resources / Guides

- [AI and Job Pipelines Overview](https://docs.livepeer.org/v2/gateways/guides/node-pipelines/guide): What a gateway pipeline is, the three job types your gateway can route (video transcoding, AI inference, BYOC), and how pipeline selection maps to gateway type.
